items.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
items.description: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

items.title: China launches coast guard patrol east of Taiwan despite international pushback

items.title: Pope Leo's visits to Lampedusa on July 4 'symbolic'
items.description: Pope Leo XIV, who has sparred with the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, spent the Fourth of July on Saturday in the epicenter of Europe’s migration debate to honor the tens of thousands of people who have died trying to reach Europe to find freedom and prosperity. While the United States marks the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with rallies, parties and fireworks, history’s first US-born pope traveled to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa to pray at a migrant cemetery and celebrate a solemn Mass for the island's newest arrivals. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports.

items.description: A-list celebrities attend wedding at Madison Square Garden to see superstar couple tie the knot.

items.title: Germany news: AfD elects leaders amid massive protests
items.description: Party delegates of the far-right AfD are meeting in Erfurt as tens of thousands of people are protesting the event. Meanwhile, Jürgen Klopp said he was "ready" to jump in as Germany's head coach. DW has more.

items.title: Zelensky denies Russian capture of key eastern city
items.description: Ukraine still controls the strategically important eastern city of Kostiantynivka, President ​Volodymyr Zelensky and the General Staff said on Saturday, rejecting Russian claims that it has been captured. Russia's military told President ​Vladimir Putin ‌on Friday that its forces had taken ⁠control of Kostiantynivka, a target that Moscow has long sought in its advance ‌through the Donetsk region.

items.title: Rebel attacks in northern Mali: Some targets meant as 'diversions', expert says
items.description: The Malian army said Saturday that several northern towns, including Gao and Sévaré, were targeted by rebels. The statement came as a rebel group announced a new offensive to capture a town in the country's north. Andrew Lebovich, a research fellow at Clingendael's Conflict Research Unit, said some of the targets were intended more as "diversions" than as primary objectives, unlike strategically important towns such as Anefis.

items.title: Foreign rescue workers help with recovering Venezuela's quake victims
items.description: With little hope of finding loved ones alive, frustrated families of those affected by Venezuela's double earthquakes clamored for help to recover their loved one's bodies Friday as uncertainty grew over the death toll from the tragedy. Authorities on Friday reported a total of 2,645 deaths and more than 12,000 wounded from the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck the country last week, with most fatalities in the coastal town of La Guaira, where scores of residential complexes were flattened.

items.title: Trump speech at Mount Rushmore: Making it clear 'who's in and who's out', expert says
items.description: President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters. Ian Reifowitz, SUNY Distinguished Professor of history at SUNY-Empire State University, said Trump has "one goal - make himself more popular and more powerful," as he pointed to how Trump has politicised the celebrations through his speech.

items.title: World Cup 2026 : Cape Verde miss out on greatest upset
items.description: Newcomers Cape Verde almost created the upset of the century by forcing the reigning champions Argentina into extra time, unfortunately the blue sharks conceded in extra time. Argentina will therefore face Egypt in the round of 16. There was not much in it between Australia's Socceroos' and the Egyptian Pharoes, the two teams went all the way to penalties, where Egypt proved extremely efficient, scoring every single shot from the penalty spot. An early Colombian goal meanwhile proved insurmountable for Ghana, the black stars will head home without any shot on target in that knockout game. France gears up for their game against Panama, and Canada face Morocco in the first round of 16 fixture.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Meta bans millions of accounts as AI impersonates real creators
items.description: Alicia discovered a Facebook account had gained more than 500,000 followers by posting AI-generated images of her.

items.title: Exclusive-World's biggest domain seller fears India's fake site crackdown could damage internet
Does football need FIFA and its World Cup?
items.description: Though the popularity of the World Cup continues to grow, its organizing body, FIFA, has arguably never been less popular. So why does football still need FIFA? And is there any way out?

items.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
items.description: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

items.title: New Zealand's Rennie expresses 'relief' as All Blacks edge narrow win over France
items.description: The All Blacks began their 2026 Nations Championship tenure with a narrow win over France on Saturday, with freshly appointed coach Dave Rennie promising the team was only going to get better as they head into future matches.

items.title: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission
items.description: In a diplomatic note to the African Union seen by Reuters, the US has vowed to block any spending of UN funds on the AU Support ​and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM).

items.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
items.description: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.

items.title: Bihar Guv. Hasnain and MoS Margherita attend Ali Khamenei's funeral ceremony in Tehran
items.description: Former External Affairs Minister Salman Khurshid is representing the Congress party at the funeral ceremony

items.title: India-Japan relations should not target third party: China
items.description: India and Japan also expressed "serious concern" at the situation in the East China Sea and South China Sea, and opposed unilateral actions that endanger freedom of navigation as well as attempts to change the status quo by force

items.title: Watch: INDIA bloc writes to CJI over SIR, EC misconduct | Above the Fold | 03.07.2026
items.description: The episode looks at the INDIA bloc’s joint memorandum to the Chief Justice of India; the Allahabad High Court’s stance on the Places of Worship Act; Russia-Ukraine war; former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei state funeral, Uddhav Thackeray’s “Ram Raksha” protest, latest FIFA updates, and more.

items.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
items.description: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

items.title: Pope defends migrants on symbolic visit to key immigration hub Lampedusa
items.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Saturday on Italy's Lampedusa island, a major port of call for migrants who risked the perilous crossing from North Africa, in a symbolic rebuke to the anti-immigrant rhetoric coming from US and EU political factions.

items.title: Belgian museum to make colonial-era Congo mining maps public
items.description: It is working to digitise the millions of coveted archive of century-old maps, surveys, and files of mining companies within five years.

items.title: Watch: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed in NYC
items.description: The couple tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony officiated by comedian Adam Sandler at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: The digital choices shaping our children’s health
items.description: Around the world, childhood is being reprogrammed by digital technologies that shape how young people learn, play and connect. Leer más
